posts.title: Inside Adoption Chain: an auditable second-order research engine for small-cap equity
posts.summary: Turns a PM's scenario into a consequence map, finds the under-covered small-cap companies on each consequence, and drafts a one-page thesis per name where every claim links to the exact SEC filing sentence. Leverage underneath judgment, never a recommendation.

posts.title: Inside StrikeOut Bot: quantile regression and beat-the-baseline discipline for MLB strikeout prediction
posts.summary: Three LightGBM quantile regressors produce a median and a 90 percent interval, a Ridge stacker blends them against a dumb baseline, and a stratified blind test on unseen post-All-Star-Break data is the only gate that lets a model ship.

posts.title: Inside Study Quest: five architectural decisions in a mastery-gated learning app
posts.summary: A gamified, mastery-gated catch-up learning PWA built for my son. Ship for the platform you can deploy from, pick the model on cost, put the integrity boundary in the database, grade only what the model is good at, and make the reward economy a config.

posts.title: Ship Note: I built a way to watch the watch party
posts.summary: The Knicks are up 2-0 in the Finals and the Game 3 watch party got scattered across three parks. So in one evening I built a way to watch the watch party, using New York's public traffic cameras as a stand-in live feed.

posts.title: Field Note: turning NYC's public traffic cameras into a live feed
posts.summary: New York DOT puts roughly 957 traffic cameras online as still images with no key and no login. Refresh one on a timer and a still becomes a feed. How I found the right cameras for each location, faked live without the page jumping, and handled the cameras that quietly die.

posts.title: Inside Atlas: a 19-signal composition scorer for off-market real estate
posts.summary: Atlas surfaces off-market real estate leads by composing 19 distinct signal types across 568,135 parcels in Wake and Durham counties, with vision signals scored by Gemma 3 27B and a total POC spend of $54.

posts.title: Inside Marketing Bot v2: five architectural decisions in a multi-pass orchestration system
posts.summary: Five architectural decisions in a multi-pass orchestration system, the alternatives rejected, and what each teaches about FDAA judgment in high-quality-bar long-context generation. The implementation is mine. The reasoning is portable.

posts.title: Inside MES Open: five architectural decisions in an algorithmic trading system
posts.summary: Five architectural decisions in an algorithmic trading system targeting the New York open on Micro E-mini S&P 500 futures. The alternatives rejected, and what each teaches about FDAA judgment in a high-noise low-sample domain.

posts.title: Inside the corridor pipeline: Python automation for LIHTC research
posts.summary: A 15-script Python pipeline that fuses Census ACS, HUD QCT, IRS OZ, USDA Food Atlas, state DDD, and land bank inventory data on a parcel key, scores against the state QAP, and produces an LP-memo-ready output.

posts.title: Inside TrialEdge: a calibrated probability ensemble for FDA catalyst prediction
posts.summary: TrialEdge predicts FDA PDUFA catalyst outcomes. 4,032 tracked entities, 208 labeled outcomes, AUC 0.686 on the held-out set. The architecture is intentionally small, intentionally calibrated, and intentionally cheap.

posts.title: Inside the Mosaic feasibility workflow: how I underwrite CRE deals with Claude
posts.summary: The Mosaic feasibility workflow is a Claude prompt suite that produces bank-quality CRE feasibility studies in 90 minutes instead of 30 hours.

posts.title: Inside DeckTrainer: a Simply-Piano-style coaching tool for the Hercules DJControl
posts.summary: DeckTrainer is a learn-to-DJ coaching tool for the Hercules DJControl Starlight and Mix. Scope discipline, hardware-constraint surfacing, phased build plan.

posts.title: Field Note: stacking R/ECAP, QCT, OZ, and DDD for LIHTC competitive advantage
posts.summary: Five public-records distress designations on a single parcel. The Mosaic framework to stack them, and the AI architecture that compresses three weeks of research to three hours.

posts.title: Field Note: matching a $25M hospitality deal to five lenders in thirty minutes
posts.summary: Hospitality debt sourcing as constraint satisfaction followed by ranking. The architecture that compresses a week of broker research into thirty minutes.

posts.title: Field Note: the NOI projection gap and why hospitality sponsors systematically over-promise
posts.summary: A coastal hospitality asset has an appraisal-stabilized NOI and a sponsor pro forma that differ by 17 percent. The gap decomposes into four components.

posts.title: Field Note: reading a brokerage flyer through an AI underwriting lens
posts.summary: A brokerage marketing flyer is the highest-volume unstructured document in CRE. Four lines actually matter. The AI-architected workflow that closes the gap.

posts.title: Field Note: packaging an emerging sponsor for institutional capital
posts.summary: An emerging sponsor faces a structural problem in the capital stack: senior debt is reachable, LP equity is the wall. The four packaging patterns institutional LPs evaluate against.

posts.title: Field Note: the Obsidian-Claude Code loop for project tracking and roadmaps
posts.summary: Obsidian as the human-facing planning layer, Claude Code as the execution layer, both reading the same file system. Conventions, failure modes, what changes at team scale.

posts.title: Field Note: Discord as the second screen for Claude Code activity
posts.summary: Discord webhooks for AI alerts: ship notifications, build failures, long-task completions, daily summaries. Hook architecture and channel layout.

posts.title: Field Note: Perplexity deep research as the synthesis layer for binary and trinary decision trees
posts.summary: Map the decision as a tree of binary or trinary branches, write a per-node Perplexity prompt, let the model synthesize, then come back to the node and decide.
